author: m.kalinin
content: 20% adversarial stake can equivocate and if the support is < 60% then proposer boost would be enough to reorg a block. So, 60% should be safe, but not less?

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I was looking at some blocks in devnet-3 and I found these slots to be interesting:
slot 65268(https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-3.ethpandaops.io/slot/65268) built by the builder extended the payload of slot 65266(https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-3.ethpandaops.io/slot/65266) but ~95% of the PTC votes for the payload availability of slot 65266 are absent. But the builder still was able to successfully extend on slot 65266 and that block got finalized. 
This is because, if the proposer extends the parent payload then regardless of ptc votes, the chain will accept it if i m right?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: even with the honest ptc reorg feature, this will be attested by nodes because only the proposer reorgs but not attesters?

author: potuz
content: yes, we need to change this behavior by https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5210

author: potuz
content: People here know more about p2p limits than I do, do we have a libp2p max size for payload envelopes set anywhere?
